author: @lewiss8629
text: Fantastic job mate! It was great meeting you at my work the other day, glad to see it all came together so nicely! :D

author: @Teknopottu
text: Great job! Now car speakers for the sounds on left and right panels driven by bluetooth amp?

author: @gojohnniegogo
text: Awesome build. End result looks wonderful!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you mate! :)

author: @NorthernKitty
text:
You have every reason to be proud, that's a genuine righteous-looking build!!  😋  
You've got me looking around my house to figure out whether I actually have room anywhere for a tabletop arcade, LOL!!  😊

I think I heard you mention you were using a Raspberry Pi 2?  I don't think that comes standard with bluetooth, but if you buy a bluetooth dongle (around $10 USD) you'd be able to easily set up sound with bluetooth speakers!!  😎  It's not a true arcade until it's waking up everyone in the house with laser blasts and 8-bit music!!!  LOL!!  😁

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text:
Thank you so much! It took me a while to figure out where it's going but now the arcade has its own place to live lol! It doesn't come with Bluetooth standard but I do have 2 15w speakers that plug into the aux of the Pi. That should do the job nicely! 
If I don't wake everyone with the word FATALITY I'm doing it wrong lol. Thanks! :)

replies.author: @NorthernKitty
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey Fantastic!!  You know... I think I can ALMOST hear it here across the pond...  😋

author: @TheMan1510
text: That looks awesome, Luke. Well done!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thanks mate! :)

author: @czechyorker
text: Even though you didn't use Raspberry Pi4 which tends to get very hot I would still use CPU Cooling for the Raspberry pi processor. Raspberry Pi 3B+ still can get hot which reduces the lifespan of the R Pi. It costs a little and you wouldn't regret it. Congratulations on this finished project. Great design. Nicely done.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you for the suggestion! I found a case online with a heatsink for £6 I think it is definitely worth it to try and prolong the life. Thanks again! :)

author: @rubartknas7075
text: Great project.Great host.Well done-mission accomplished....you levelled up on that one, giana bro, what a great and giant screen you have there. Now , please invite Robert to have some fun in   2player mode  with the amazing "arcade wizard".

author: @make425
text:
Any thoughts on speakers?
Old blue tooth! 🤔
Very nice build Luke 👍👍

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yes I completely forgot them! I have two 15w speakers mounted. Just not wired up lol. They will be plugged into the aux on the PI. Thanks mate! :)

replies.author: @make425
replies.text:
I don't even know how i noticed it 😂. Figured it wouldn't take long playing with it before you noticed rob was making all the kick and punch noises 🤣. 
Nice build never the less. And still two 👍👍up. 😎

author: @roberthopgood1894
text: Is there a way to select different games

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
